曼聯終極FF轉會新聞暨吹水區FAQ！

想知去Old Trafford朝聖，係邊度買波飛最平最有保證﹖

想知係Old Trafford睇波，坐邊個看台最爽﹖

想知去Carrington訓練基地度等班球員攞簽名同合照應該點做﹖

想知新球季係邊度買波衫最平、印字最好﹖

想知點寄信去曼聯官方問佢攞球星相同簽名﹖

同埋想知一啲遊覽曼徹斯特市小Tips﹖

詳情請click入https://goo.gl/wLBkmb。另歡迎各位有興趣留啲心得分享比巴打們嘅巴打，係以上嘅link度留下備註，小弟會幫手加落篇文度(記得加番自己個會員名，方便credit你!)。大家亦可以係曼po度留言，我見到嘅話亦會加番落個google doc度。如果1001開po嘅巴打覺得啱洗，歡迎將啲FAQ或者呢一段文字放係第二個comment度，方便大家查閱。覺得冇需要嘅請ignore。特別感謝【Berbatov】、【黑白灰......】、【特西】、【活華特】、【SNOOZE】、【朗隊】以及【曼聯聯盟2】之巴打提供心得。

隊波比太多閪人教過， 已經冇哂form可言， 本身諗住有咩摩二季， 點知踢出黎都係咁。

其實摩狗早啲走係好， 歷史證明佢係即食，教完隊波會好撚殘， 啲有前途既技術球員都會廢或者賣哂。

同埋大家唔好唔記得仲有樣嘢叫摩三季， 搵定教練都岩

當初請佢就係想即食同唔預佢長教啦…
你要長遠兼快有成績 但又唔等得…
仲要你球會前任教練都唔同風格

replies.msg:
連加特穿14 號球衣竟因為他??

在前季穿上35 號球衣而成功打入曼聯一隊，並且在足總杯決賽射入奠定勝局一球的中場連加特，今季放棄了該件「大冧把」的球衣，改為披上14 號球衣上陣。連加特於接受曼聯官網訪問時，透露了當中的原因。

該名24 歲的英格蘭國腳表示:「我在小時候常常觀看阿仙奴名宿亨利比賽，知道他射入了無數的入球，我自小便以他作榜樣，所以我覺得14 號是個好的號碼。」

除了前季在英足總杯決賽入球外，連加特亦在上季的聯賽杯決賽中入球。連加特分別把該件35 號及14 號球衣封存好，並交給其他球員簽名，以作留念。「我這樣做主要是給自己做個紀念，可能將來會找個地方把它們展示出來。」

對於儲球衣的習慣，連加特說「我再年青一點時，所收集球衣的數目更多，但現在主要收集跟我對賽的朋友、或我認識的人的球衣。」

當被問到現在最想收集誰的球衣時，連加特說:「我想是美斯或恩尼斯達吧。」

連加特要繼續努力，希望他下季可以在更多重要賽事，甚至歐聯決賽入球，令他的球衣收藏大增！

Manchester United news: Paul Pogba 'misses' Juventus, says former teammate Paulo Dybala

Pogba returned to United in a world record £89m transfer in the summer, but Dybala says he is missing life in Turin after leaving his teammates

Paul Pogba may finally be finding his form at Manchester United following his £89m world record transfer this summer, but it appears life may not be all that rosy for the most expensive player in the world.

His former Juventus teammate, Paulo Dybala, has spoken publicly to admit that while the Italian club misses the France midfielder on and off the pitch, he is also missing life in Italy after returning to Manchester following four years in Turin.

Pogba’s second stint in the Premier League got off to a slow start, but his performances in recent weeks have picked up noticeably along with United’s, and they were unlucky to draw games against Arsenal, West Ham and Everton before reeling off three consecutive wins this month.

The 23-year-old has slotted in seamlessly at a club he knows very well, and immediately rekindled his friendship with the likes of Jesse Lingard with the two forming a close bond during their early years at Old Trafford.

But it seem that’s Pogba is still missing some aspects of life in Italy, with Argentina international Dybala discussing the absence of Pogba in Turin this season.

“I’m still talking to Pogba, we are missing him for his quality on the pitch and his jokes in the dressing room. However, he misses us as well,” Dybala told Italian newspaper Corriere dello Sport.

The striker, who scored 19 goals in Serie A last season, has only found the back of the net three times this term, though that has not stopped Juventus once again rising to the top of the table with a four-point lead over Juventus.

Dybala used their dominance in Italy to poke fun at United this season, with Jose Mourinho’s side currently sixth in the Premier League table, and an alarming 13 points behind Chelsea to leave their title hopes on the verge of being over before the turn of the year.

“Just look at our position in the table and the one of Manchester United,” Dybala added, hinting that United’s form may have something to do with Pogba’s apparent longing to be back in Turin.

在2013年他在更衣室摩連奴質疑C朗防守不力，二人當面對質火藥味濃。最後Ｃ朗被隊友拉住沒有身體接觸，二人正式決裂，摩連奴同年亦離開了皇馬。

replies.msg:
(星島日報報道)摩連奴與C朗拿度於皇家馬德里共事卻反目成仇，記者迪亞高托利斯的新書《摩連奴不為人知的世界》就披露了當中來龍去脈。原來兩人於一○至一一歐聯四強對巴塞隆拿一役，互相指罵導致師徒決裂。

　　「狂人」與C朗反目成仇的因由一直是一個謎，不過迪亞高托利斯編寫的新書《摩連奴不為人知的世界》就「大爆」箇中原因。於一○至一一歐聯四強次回合皇馬作客巴塞前，C朗曾出言批評摩連奴首回合戰術保守：「我們踢得保守，都是因為你！」

　　書中描述摩連奴隨即還擊：「因為你不去防守，因此我要讓全隊回防去遷就你！」結果，皇馬兩回合以1：3落敗，兩人關係亦破裂。

【now.com體育】據英國傳媒報道，曼聯領隊擔心隊中前鋒盧卡古會因為過於集中健身，失去了原有的速度。

據英國《太陽報》報道，比利時前鋒盧卡古（Romelu Lukaku）今季轉投曼聯後，身形日趨龐大，不過經曼聯的隊醫檢查後，證實盧卡古變得身形巨大的原因是肌肉的增長，而不是肥胖。不過領隊摩連奴擔心這位24歲射手肌肉過剩的話，會影響他速度上的優勢。

盧卡古今季轉投曼聯後原本有一個很出色的開始，首10比賽射入11個入球。不過自從他加強肌肉訓練後，入球產量反而大幅下滑，及後的12場比賽僅射入1球，部份球迷已經開始對盧卡古的表現失去耐性，而摩連奴早前亦曾經表示盧卡古的跑動範圍不足。

曼聯近兩仗對李斯特城和般尼都接連失分，雖然現時仍緊守英超第2位，但已經落後榜首的曼城15分之多。摩連奴上場試用伊巴謙莫域伙拍盧卡古的「雙塔」陣式，不過效果依然不大，最後只能憑後備入替的連格梅開二度逼和般尼。

仲練肌肉做乜鳩

quote.msg:
【now.com體育】據英國傳媒報道，曼聯領隊擔心隊中前鋒盧卡古會因為過於集中健身，失去了原有的速度。

據英國《太陽報》報道，比利時前鋒盧卡古（Romelu Lukaku）今季轉投曼聯後，身形日趨龐大，不過經曼聯的隊醫檢查後，證實盧卡古變得身形巨大的原因是肌肉的增長，而不是肥胖。不過領隊摩連奴擔心這位24歲射手肌肉過剩的話，會影響他速度上的優勢。

盧卡古今季轉投曼聯後原本有一個很出色的開始，首10比賽射入11個入球。不過自從他加強肌肉訓練後，入球產量反而大幅下滑，及後的12場比賽僅射入1球，部份球迷已經開始對盧卡古的表現失去耐性，而摩連奴早前亦曾經表示盧卡古的跑動範圍不足。

曼聯近兩仗對李斯特城和般尼都接連失分，雖然現時仍緊守英超第2位，但已經落後榜首的曼城15分之多。摩連奴上場試用伊巴謙莫域伙拍盧卡古的「雙塔」陣式，不過效果依然不大，最後只能憑後備入替的連格梅開二度逼和般尼。

Manchester United tracking Napoli midfielder Jorginho

Manchester United are monitoring Napoli midfielder Jorginho as they ponder a replacement for Michael Carrick.

M.E.N. Sport understands United are admirers of the 26-year-old Italy international and have shortlisted Jorginho as a potential midfield recruit ahead of next season. United have already scouted Borussia Dortmund duo Julian Weigl and Christian Pulisic.

Holding midfielder Jorginho scored in Napoli's 4-2 Champions League defeat to Manchester City and was dubbed 'wonderful' by Pep Guardiola, who dubiously claimed the Neapolitans were 'perhaps the best I've faced in my career'. Jorginho has started 15 of Napoli's 18 Serie A fixtures in defensive midfield, scoring twice and providing three assists.

A Napoli website claimed on Wednesday the Italian leaders had received a 'big offer' from an English club. The M.E.N. revealed last week United manager Jose Mourinho wants to sign a central midfielder in the January transfer window but Jorginho would be cup-tied in Europe, having played in the Champions League.

United captain Carrick has not played since September 20 after he underwent a heart procedure. The 36-year-old was last included in a matchday squad at Southampton on September 23 and his contract with the club expires at the end of the season.

Mourinho has already confirmed Carrick is free to join his coaching staff should he decide to retire at the end of the campaign.

"Michael as a person is more important than Michael as a player," Mourinho said last month. "We gave him the time to relax, to recover, to feel confident, to make the decision, wants to play, wants to stop.

"He knows my coaching staff has a chair for him if he wants, when he wants, the chair is there in the office for him. I want that, the board wants that, the owners want that, so Michael is in a comfortable situation of his future will be with us.

"But he wants to be a player until the end of the season and now he feels confident to be back. So training, one more option for us, we need players and if Michael wants I'm happy with that."

Marouane Fellaini, 30, rejected United's offer of a new contract in September and is approaching the last six months on his deal. Fellaini is free to negotiate with other clubs from next week but it is understood United will resist selling him in the winter window.

Fellaini was the first of seven central midfield signings United made after Sir Alex Ferguson refused to sign one during his last six years in charge.

Fellaini, Ander Herrera, Daley Blind, Morgan Schneiderlin, Bastian Schweinsteiger, Paul Pogba and Nemanja Matic arrived for a combined £226million. Schneiderlin and Schweinsteiger left last year while Blind's appearance in midfield at Bristol City earlier this month was his first there since the 2015 FA Cup quarter-final defeat to Arsenal.

Jorginho is viewed as a more mobile holding option than the 29-year-old Matic and a specialist in the role as opposed to Herrera, who was converted to a more defensive-minded midfielder by Mourinho last season.

Brazil-born Jorginho elected to represent Italy last year and started in the goalless World Cup play off second leg against Sweden, who prevented the Azzurri from qualifying for their first World Cup finals since 1958.

Carlo Sartori became the first Italian to play for United in 1968. Massimo Taibi, Giuseppe Rossi, Federico Macheda and Matteo Darmian are the only other Italians who have played for United.

Barcelona fear Virgil van Dijk's big money move to Liverpool could force Manchester clubs into paying Samuel Umtiti's £50million release clause

Barcelona are growing increasingly concerned that one of either Manchester United or City will pay Samuel Umtiti's release clause after Virgil van Dijk's big money move to Liverpool caused chaos in the transfer market.

On Wednesday night it was announced that Van Dijk will be heading to Anfield for £75million on January 1, the highest fee ever-paid for a defender.

According to Don Balon, the La Liga giants are now worried this will spark United or City into paying Umtiti’s £50million clause in order to boost their own defensive options.

The French defender has been a Nou Camp regular since his £21m move from Lyon in the summer of 2016, making 62 appearances.

Ernesto Valverde is reportedly keen to offer Umtiti a new contract to stave off Premier League interest, but it remains to be seen if he will sign.

United had identified Umtiti as one of their top transfer targets with Jose Mourinho plotting to take advantage of his modest release clause.

The United boss wants to bring him to Old Trafford after his summer signing Victor Lindelof's failure to kick on following his move from Benfica.

Eric Bailly is also out with an ankle problem for three months and Umtiti would act as an upgrade on any other United central defender.

City may also be in the market for another centre back after captain Vincent Kompany suffered yet another injury setback on Wednesday night.

Kompany limped off less than 10 minutes into City's Premier League clash with Newcastle United at St James' Park on Wednesday night with a calf problem.

Cristiano Ronaldo puts Manchester United and PSG on alert as he asks Real Madrid to sell him for €100m

Cristiano Ronaldo has asked that Real Madrid set their selling price for a potential summer transfer at €100million (£89million) or less.

At the same time, the World Player of the Year's representatives have made it known to Paris Saint-Germain and Manchester United that the forward is willing to “fight for a move” out of Spanish football if necessary.

The release clause in the five-season contract Ronaldo signed in November 2016 stands at a symbolic €1billion (£887million). Ronaldo's camp are seeking a commitment from Madrid president Florentino Perez that he would sell for a "realistic fee" of lower than one tenth of that sum, enabling long-term suitors PSG and United to pursue the Portugal international.

Ronaldo's relationship with Perez is at a dangerously low ebb, with sources close to the most successful footballer in Madrid's history saying that it has a reached a point “he doesn't even want to talk to the president”.

Central to the 32-year-old's discontent is his belief that Perez reneged on an end-of-season promise to improve his financial terms relative to the sport's other leading protagonists.

Although Ronaldo cemented his status as the game's best player by leading Madrid to a Liga, Champions League and FIFA Club World Cup treble this year while collecting back-to-back World Player of the Year awards, his basic salary has been eclipsed by both Lionel Messi and Neymar .

Ronaldo's argument is that his on-field achievements should be correctly reflected in his pay – not least because his commercial value to Madrid is superior to that of the two South Americans.

“I'm the best player in history, in the good moments and the bad ones,” said Ronaldo in a pointed interview with France Football to mark this year's Ballon d'Or success.

“I respect everyone’s preferences, but I’ve never seen anyone better than me. I have always thought that. No footballer can do the things I can. There’s no player more complete than me. I play well with both feet, I’m quick, powerful, good with the head, I score goals, I make assists. There are guys who prefer Neymar or Messi. But I tell you: There’s no-one more complete than me.

“No-one has won as many individual trophies as me. And I’m not speaking just of the Ballon d’Or. That says something, doesn’t it? It’s not just a result of the work I do in the gym as some people think. It’s the sum of many things. Legends like Floyd Mayweather and LeBron James don’t get to their perfect level by chance. Many factors coincide. To be at the top and to stay there you have to have more talent than the others.”

Ronaldo is also understood to have been angered by what he regarded as a lack of respect on Perez's part at the Ballon d'Or ceremony in Paris earlier this month.

At an event held to honour the Portugal international's fifth such award, Madrid's president chose to publicly flirt with Neymar. "Being in Madrid would make it easier to win the Ballon d'Or," said Perez of the PSG forward."Real Madrid is a club which gives all you need as a big player, everyone knows that I already wanted to sign him at one point."

Madrid lost a bidding war to Barcelona when Neymar left Santos in 2013. Although the Brazil international deployed a £197million release clause to join Paris Saint-Germain last summer, Neymar's father has encouraged Madrid to renew their pursuit of the 25-year-old – a move Ronaldo's camp are well aware of.

After scoring the only goal of this month's Club World Cup Final against Gremio the following week, Ronaldo placed public pressure on Perez by stating a desire to finish his career at the Santiago Bernabeu – if conditions were correct.

"I would really like to, but things don't depend on me," Ronaldo said. “What depends on me is what I do on the pitch. I am not the one who runs the club, that is for those in charge. My job is on the pitch and I think I am doing it well, the rest I can't control."

Ronaldo's current deal runs until the summer of 2021 and includes a guaranteed gross salary of £43million. That figure was surpassed first by PSG's summer deal with Neymar, and then by Messi's renewal with Barca.

The Argentina international became the first footballer to earn the Sterling equivalent of over £1million as a basic weekly wage on a contract that is understood to be worth £44million a year, after tax.

Ronaldo's situation is further complicated by an ongoing dispute with the Spanish government over an alleged £13million of unpaid tax. The player denies evading tax or hiding details of his income and argues that he has been targeted because of his celebrity. “If I wasn’t called Cristiano Ronaldo, I wouldn’t be sat here,” he said in a court appearance earlier this year.

Asked about Ronaldo's future last week, Madrid coach Zinedine Zidane said: "What I think is that Cristiano - what he has done and what he is doing - deserves all of the club's respect. But only after the winter break will we talk about it."

Although the Brazil international deployed a £197million release clause to join Paris Saint-Germain last summer, Neymar's father has encouraged Madrid to renew their pursuit of the 25-year-old – a move Ronaldo's camp are well aware of.

After scoring the only goal of this month's Club World Cup Final against Gremio the following week, Ronaldo placed public pressure on Perez by stating a desire to finish his career at the Santiago Bernabeu – if conditions were correct.

"I would really like to, but things don't depend on me," Ronaldo said. “What depends on me is what I do on the pitch. I am not the one who runs the club, that is for those in charge. My job is on the pitch and I think I am doing it well, the rest I can't control."

Ronaldo's current deal runs until the summer of 2021 and includes a guaranteed gross salary of £43million. That figure was surpassed first by PSG's summer deal with Neymar, and then by Messi's renewal with Barca.

The Argentina international became the first footballer to earn the Sterling equivalent of over £1million as a basic weekly wage on a contract that is understood to be worth £44million a year, after tax.

Ronaldo's situation is further complicated by an ongoing dispute with the Spanish government over an alleged £13million of unpaid tax. The player denies evading tax or hiding details of his income and argues that he has been targeted because of his celebrity. “If I wasn’t called Cristiano Ronaldo, I wouldn’t be sat here,” he said in a court appearance earlier this year.

Asked about Ronaldo's future last week, Madrid coach Zinedine Zidane said: "What I think is that Cristiano - what he has done and what he is doing - deserves all of the club's respect. But only after the winter break will we talk about it."

但要聯賽贏鏝 基本上好難 因為控球勁到令對方節奏控制係自己手入面 要快要慢都得 咁打聯賽下游一定冇防反咁辛苦

當然你可以拎入波失波數據黎打飛機
